conditions.evidence_text: In 69 wt-% acetonitrile–water PEG is sufficiently close to critical conditions (Fig. 1).

conditions.evidence_text: A separation of the non-polar PCL under critical conditions for the polar EO unit can only be possible on reversed phases. On typical RP columns, a CAP for EO exists in 85–95 wt-% methanol–water [60]; in acetone–water two critical points have been observed: at 26–30 wt-% and at 90–93 wt-% (the latter exists only on silica–based stationary phases) [60, 61]. In 90 wt-% methanol–water low molecular PCL samples could be reasonably separated, but higher PCLs are not soluble in such a mobile phase. PCL is also insoluble in 30% acetone, and in 90% acetone the retention of PCL is not sufficient. On RP phases, critical conditions for EO exist also in acetonitrile–water. We have found that PEG and PEG-MME elute close to the CAP in quite a wide range of mobile phase composition: in 69 wt-% acetonitrile–water PEG is sufficiently close to critical conditions (Fig. 1).
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria
